source: Greenbarz physical market intel · Aggregated from broker chatter, port call data, and trader network sources. Refreshed weekly during active trial period, then quarterly.
note: Mideast-Asia crude flow pulse. We track who's buying, what's moving, and where the heat is — not individual cargo tickets. Aggregated by week and grade family for signal clarity.
scope: Mideast Sour Crude · Asian Buyers · 6-week rolling window

mood.label: ACTIVE
mood.headline: Heavy week for Mideast sours — premiums firming, Asian buyers leaning in

deal_signals.title: Deal Tape · what we're seeing
deal_signals.subtitle: 6-8 representative signals · not individual trade tickets
deal_signals.method: Generalized observations across multiple deals. We don't publish counterparty pairs or exact prices — we publish what the flow is telling us.

cards.headline: Murban premiums firming hard
cards.body: Multiple cargoes printing $4-25 over Dated Brent for May-Jun loadings. Korean and Taiwanese refiners bidding aggressively. Light sweet preference back in fashion.

cards.headline: Asian buyers active on US Midland
cards.body: Taiwan (Formosa, CPC), Korea (SK), and Japan (Cosmo, Idemitsu) all picking up WTI Midland for Jul-26 arrival. Brent-WTI arb wide enough to make this work.

cards.headline: Dubai partials trading at offer
cards.body: Three June Dubai partials cleared at the upper end of bid/offer — sellers holding out and getting paid. Asian demand strength is visible all the way through to physical convergences.

cards.headline: Oman M1/M3 spread blowing out
cards.body: Front-month Oman widening sharply over deferred. Either Asian refiners are short prompt barrels or Mideast supply is getting tight near-term. Either way, bullish pulse.

cards.headline: West Africa light sweet thin
cards.body: Bonny, Escravos, Qua Iboe cargoes loading but no aggressive bids reported. Indian refiners taking opportunistic volumes. Atlantic Basin lights trading in line, not hot.

cards.headline: Heavy sour appetite cooling
cards.body: Basrah Heavy and Qatar Marine seeing softer bid interest. Asian refiners shifting toward lighter grades — likely chasing better gasoline yields into summer driving season.

gb_take.title: GB Read
gb_take.body: Mideast sours are bid hard and Asian refiners are paying premium for light sweet — that's a coordinated signal. Murban's M1/M3 widening, Dubai partials at offer, and Korean/Taiwan term buying all point the same direction: prompt physical tightness in Asia. Watch the OPEC+ communications next 2-3 weeks. If they signal compliance hold, this premium structure has legs into Q3. If a hike leaks, fade fast.
